description.en: Castile's deliberately dense bread: a stiff, singularly low-hydration dough (around 50%) repeatedly rolled and folded through a press until satin-smooth, instead of kneaded. The result is a tight, chewy crumb under a smooth, shiny crust that keeps for days rather than hours.
description.de: Kastiliens bewusst dichtes Brot: ein steifer Teig mit niedriger Hydration (rund 50%), der statt geknetet immer wieder durch eine Presse gerollt und gefaltet wird, bis er seidig glatt ist. Das Ergebnis: feste Krume unter glänzender Kruste, die tagelang statt Stunden hält.
description.es: El pan deliberadamente denso de Castilla: una masa firme, de hidratación singularmente baja (en torno al 50%), pasada una y otra vez por el refinado en vez de amasarse, hasta quedar sedosa. El resultado es una miga apretada bajo una corteza lisa y brillante que aguanta días.
description.fr: Le pain volontairement dense de Castille: une pâte ferme, à l'hydratation singulièrement basse (environ 50%), roulée et pliée à répétition au lieu d'être pétrie, jusqu'à devenir soyeuse. Le résultat: une mie compacte sous une croûte lisse et brillante qui se garde des jours.

origin.note: The 'sobado' or 'bregado' rolling technique, rather than kneading, is what gives this bread its uniquely tight, non-porous crumb.

ingredients.note: 300 g harina panadera plus 200 g harina panadera recia (strong wheat flour), in the final dough

yield.shape: flat torta, higher at the center, cross-scored on top

keeping.note: Low hydration and a dense, sealed crumb are exactly why candeal keeps far longer than a typical high-hydration loaf; bakers historically prized it for durability.

method.title: Prefermento
method.summary: The day before, mix flour, water, salt and a trace of yeast into a firm dough; let it rise, then refrigerate overnight.

method.title: Final dough
method.summary: Combine the prefermento with the remaining flours, water, yeast and salt into a very stiff dough, famously low-hydration, only about 50%.

method.title: The refinado
method.summary: Instead of ordinary kneading, repeatedly pass the dough through a pasta roller (or under a heavy roller), folding it in half each time, until it turns smooth and satiny.

method.title: Shape
method.summary: Press into a flat torta, higher in the center, and mark with the traditional cross-scoring and a pattern of pin-pricks so it can't over-expand.

method.title: Proof
method.summary: Let it rise about an hour at a warm room temperature.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake at 190°C for 15 minutes, then 170°C for 20 minutes more, until the surface is smooth, shiny and pale gold.

source.adaptation: Grams follow the source's own stated aggregate totals (600 g flour / 300 g water / 10 g salt / 3 g yeast).
